subject: Gaming
title: AI Art in Games: The Ethics Question
description: A micro-lesson that makes AI Art in Games: The Ethics Question usable.

hook.text: AI can generate game art in seconds. Artists who spent years training fear being replaced by algorithms.

buildup.text: AI art tools like Midjourney and Stable Diffusion can create game-quality assets at near-zero cost.

discovery.text: AI art is trained on millions of human artworks—often without consent or compensation for the original artists.

twist.text: Some indie studios use AI art to compete with larger teams. Others refuse on ethical grounds. The split is real.

climax.text: AI art in games forces the industry to decide what it values: efficiency or human creative labor.

punchline.text: Trained on human art without consent. Faster and cheaper. But is it ethical?

quiz.question: What ethical concern surrounds AI-generated art in games?
options: AI is trained on human artwork often without consent or compensation
AI art is always lower quality
